<section_header_level_1><loc_35><loc_60><loc_151><loc_67>2.2.4 I$^{2}$C interface pins</section_header_level_1>
<otsl><loc_83><loc_87><loc_464><loc_137><fcel>Name<fcel>Description<nl><fcel>SCL<fcel>I$^{2}$C clock, need external pull-up<nl><fcel>SDA<fcel>I$^{2}$C data, need external pull-up<nl><fcel>ALERT<fcel>I$^{2}$C interrupt, need external pull-up<nl><fcel>ADDR0, ADDR1<fcel>I$^{2}$C device address bits (see Section 4 I$^{2}$C Interface)<nl><caption><loc_223><loc_78><loc_324><loc_85>Table 3. I$^{2}$C interface pin list</caption></otsl>
<section_header_level_1><loc_83><loc_145><loc_135><loc_151>· Warning:</section_header_level_1>
<unordered_list><list_item><loc_106><loc_154><loc_444><loc_160>-ADDR0 and ADDR1 pins must be connected to ground when there is no connection to an MCU</list_item>
<list_item><loc_106><loc_162><loc_360><loc_168>-SCL and SDA are pulled down when none of VDD or VSYS is present</list_item>
</unordered_list>
<section_header_level_1><loc_35><loc_177><loc_110><loc_184>2.2.5 DISCH</section_header_level_1>
<text><loc_83><loc_186><loc_459><loc_199>This input/output pin can be used to implement a discharge path for highly capacitive V$_{BUS}$ line on power system side.</text>
<text><loc_83><loc_201><loc_462><loc_214>When used as input, the discharge is internal and a serial resistor must connected to the pin to limit the discharge current through the pin. Maximum discharge current is 500 mA.</text>
<text><loc_83><loc_216><loc_437><loc_229>The pin can be also used as an open drain output to control an external V$_{BUS}$ discharge path when higher discharge current is required by the application, for instance.</text>
<text><loc_83><loc_231><loc_447><loc_250>The pin is activated at the same time as the internal discharge path on VBUS$_{VS_DISCH}$ pin. The discharge is activated automatically during cable disconnection, transition to a lower PDO voltage, hard reset and error recovery state. The discharge time is programmable by NVM (see Section 5 Start-up configuration).</text>
<section_header_level_1><loc_35><loc_258><loc_102><loc_265>2.2.6 GND</section_header_level_1>
<text><loc_83><loc_267><loc_111><loc_273>Ground.</text>
<section_header_level_1><loc_35><loc_282><loc_117><loc_289>2.2.7 ATTACH</section_header_level_1>
<text><loc_83><loc_291><loc_457><loc_303>This pin is asserted when a valid source-to-sink connection is established. It is also asserted when a connection to a debug accessory device is detected.</text>
<page_footer><loc_35><loc_488><loc_81><loc_492>DS12499 - Rev 8</page_footer>
